frameworks.name: reward prediction error
frameworks.one_line: standard RPE signal gated by inferred causal structure of environment

frameworks.name: Bayesian causal inference
frameworks.one_line: beliefs about whether outcomes are self- or externally-caused shape learning

frameworks.name: credit assignment
frameworks.one_line: attributing outcomes to own actions versus external causes during learning

core_question: Which neural circuits represent causal-belief-modulated reward prediction errors during reinforcement learning?
key_assumptions: beliefs about causal structure modulate RPEs during action value updating
credit assignment is best explained by Bayesian RL
causal beliefs are inferable from observable task structure
manipulations: task manipulates beliefs about causal structure of the environment
fMRI during behavioral task in human participants
key_findings: causal-belief-modulated RPEs represented in posterior putamen
standard unmodulated RPEs represented in ventral striatum
causal-structure beliefs represented in anterior insula and inferior frontal gyrus
effective connectivity from anterior insula to posterior putamen
interpretations: causal beliefs in anterior insula integrate with prediction errors in posterior putamen to update action values
corticostriatal learning is gated by causal inference
